Returns the string (usually a single character) corresponding to the character code indicated by the specified number.
This is always a single Unicode character, but for practical reasons, Unicode supplementary characters (where Number is in the range 0x10000 to 0x10FFFF) are counted as two characters.
If Number is 0, the return value is a string containing a binary null character, not an empty (zero-length) string. This can be safely assigned to a variable, passed to a function or concatenated with another string.
Clicks/holds a mouse button, scrolls the wheel, moves the mouse.

[Options] are a combination of the following: Specify the X/Y coords where the click should happen. Click '100 200' Specify which button: Left|L|Right|R|Middle|M|X1|X2 Click 'Left' ('Left' can be omitted in most cases) Specify click count: Click '2' (2 left clicks - doubleclick) Click '100 200 2' (2 left clicks at specified coords) Click '100 200 Right 2' (2 right clicks ar specified coords) Up or Down: Click 'Down' (clicks left button and holds it) Click 'Right Down' (clicks right button and holds it) Relative Click '100 200 Right Rel (clicks right button at specified coords, but relative to current mouse pos)

Retrieves type information from a COM object.

COM uses the following values to identify basic data types: VT_EMPTY := 0 ; No value VT_NULL := 1 ; SQL-style Null VT_I2 := 2 ; 16-bit signed int VT_I4 := 3 ; 32-bit signed int VT_R4 := 4 ; 32-bit floating-point number VT_R8 := 5 ; 64-bit floating-point number VT_CY := 6 ; Currency VT_DATE := 7 ; Date VT_BSTR := 8 ; COM string (Unicode string with length prefix) VT_DISPATCH := 9 ; COM object VT_ERROR := 0xA ; Error code (32-bit integer) VT_BOOL := 0xB ; Boolean True (-1) or False (0) VT_VARIANT := 0xC ; VARIANT (must be combined with VT_ARRAY or VT_BYREF) VT_UNKNOWN := 0xD ; IUnknown interface pointer VT_DECIMAL := 0xE ; (not supported) VT_I1 := 0x10 ; 8-bit signed int VT_UI1 := 0x11 ; 8-bit unsigned int VT_UI2 := 0x12 ; 16-bit unsigned int VT_UI4 := 0x13 ; 32-bit unsigned int VT_I8 := 0x14 ; 64-bit signed int VT_UI8 := 0x15 ; 64-bit unsigned int VT_INT := 0x16 ; Signed machine int VT_UINT := 0x17 ; Unsigned machine int VT_RECORD := 0x24 ; User-defined type -- NOT SUPPORTED VT_ARRAY := 0x2000 ; SAFEARRAY VT_BYREF := 0x4000 ; Pointer to another type of value
Retrieves the value or pointer stored in a COM wrapper object.

Specify the full text or just the leading part of the text of the entry or page to select.
If the text matches multiple entries or pages, the first one is selected.
The search is not case-sensitive. For example, if a list box contains the entry 'UNIX Text', specifying the word unix (lowercase) would be enough to select it.

Control-or-pos: ControlClick 'x100 y200', ... ControlClick Var, ... (where Var is ClassNN, text, HWND, or an obj with 'hwnd' property) Button: Left (L), Right (R), Middle (M), X1, X2, WheelUp (WU), WheelDown (WD)
ClickCount: Default = 1
Options: NA = possible reliability improvement D = press and hold mouse button U = release specified mouse button Pos = use only 'x y' for Control-or-pos param Xn = where to click, relative to control top left (default is center) Yn = where to click, relative to control top left (default is center)

Specify the full text of the entry to find.
The search is not case-sensitive.
For example, if a list box contains the entry 'UNIX', specifying the word unix (lowercase) would find it.

Value: Add '+0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle | Value Remove '-0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle & ~Value Toggle '^0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle ^ Value

Value: Add '+0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle | Value Remove '-0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle & ~Value Toggle '^0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle ^ Value

Shows the drop-down list of a ComboBox control.
Sets coordinate mode for built-in functions.

TargetType: ToolTip, Pixel, Mouse, Caret, Menu
RelativeTo: Screen (default), Window, Client
Returns: the previous setting.

By default, coordinates are relative to the active window's client area. This applies to all built-in functions that use coordinates, except those documented otherwise (e.g. WinMove and InputBox).
Every newly launched thread starts off fresh with the default setting for this function. That default may be changed by using this function during script startup.
The built-in A_CoordMode variables contain the current settings and can also be assigned a new value instead of calling CoordMode.

DateTime = YYYYMMDDHH24MISS format Time = Integer or float. TimeUnits = Seconds (S), Minutes (M), Hours (H), Days (D) Year (if used) must be on or after 1601.
Compares two date-time values and returns the difference.
TimeUnits = Seconds (S), Minutes (M), Hours (H), Days (D) Year (if used) must be on or after 1601.

Creates a folder.

Returns: R = READONLY A = ARCHIVE S = SYSTEM H = HIDDEN D = DIRECTORY O = OFFLINE C = COMPRESSED

Overwrite: 0 = Do not overwrite 1 = Overwrite 2 = Same as 1 without 'limitation'
Rename: R = Rename instead of move
Displays a standard dialog that allows the user to select a folder.
Options: 0 = all below are disabled 1 = 'New Folder' button +2 = edit field to type folder name +4 = omit BIF_NEWDIALOGSTYLE - good for WinPE (no 'New Folder' button)
Calls a function inside a DLL, such as a standard Windows API function.
DllCall returns the actual value returned by Function. If Function is of a type that does not return a value, the result is an undefined value of the specified return type (integer by default).

Types of Arguments and Return Values: Ptr: A pointer-sized integer, equivalent to Int or Int64 if current script interpreter is 64-bit. Ptr should be used for pointers to arrays or structures (such as RECT* or LPPOINT) and almost all handles (such as HWND, HBRUSH or HBITMAP).
If the parameter is a pointer to a single numeric value such as LPDWORD or Int*, generally the * or P suffix should be used instead of 'Ptr'.
If an object is passed to a Ptr parameter, the value of the Ptr property is used.
* or P (suffix): Append an asterisk to any type (e.g. Int*) to cause the address of the argument to be passed rather than the value itself. Since the value of such an argument might be modified by the function, whenever a VarRef is passed as the argument, the variable's contents will be updated after the function returns.
In general, an asterisk is used whenever a function has an argument type or return type that starts with 'LP'.
U (prefix): Prepend the letter U to any of the integer types above to interpret it as an unsigned integer (UInt64, UInt, ...). Unsigned 64-bit integers produced by a function are not supported.
HRESULT: A 32-bit integer. This is generally used with COM functions and is valid only as a return type without any prefix or suffix. Error values (as defined by the FAILED macro) are never returned; instead, an OSError() is thrown. Therefore, the return value is a success code in the range 0 to 2147483647.
HRESULT is the default return type for ComCall.

Str: A string such as 'Blue' or MyVar, or a VarRef such as &MyVar. If the called function modifies the string and the argument is a naked variable or VarRef, its contents will be updated.
If the function is designed to store a string longer than the parameter's input value (or if the parameter is for output only), the recommended approach is to create a Buffer, use the Ptr type to pass it, and use StrGet to retrieve the string after the function returns, as in the wsprintf example.
A Str argument must not be an expression that evaluates to a number (such as i+1).
WStr: Wide string (UTF-16). By default is equivalent to Str.
AStr: Automatically convert value to ANSI.

Int64: A 64-bit integer.
Int|Long: A 32-bit integer. An Int should also be used for each BOOL argument expected by a function. An unsigned Int (UInt) is also used quite frequently, such as for DWORD.
Short: A 16-bit integer. An unsigned Short (UShort) can be used with functions that expect a WORD.
Char: An 8-bit integer. An unsigned character (UChar) can be used with functions that expect a BYTE.
Float: A 32-bit floating point number, which provides 6 digits of precision.
Double: A 64-bit floating point number, which provides 15 digits of precision.
Downloads a file from the Internet.
Ejects the tray of the specified CD/DVD drive. DriveEject can also eject a removable drive like a USB stick.
Returns the total capacity of the drive which contains the specified path, in megabytes.
Returns the type of the specified drive's file system.
Returns the volume label of the specified drive.
Returns a string of letters, one character for each drive letter in the system.
Returns the volume serial number of the specified drive.
Returns the free disk space of the drive which contains the specified path, in megabytes.
Returns the status of the drive which contains the specified path.
Returns the media status of the specified CD/DVD drive.
Returns the type of the drive which contains the specified path.
Prevents the eject feature of the specified drive from working.
Retracts the tray of the specified CD/DVD drive.
Changes the volume label of the specified drive.
Restores the eject feature of the specified drive.

Returns the line number in an Edit control where the caret (text insert point) resides.
Returns the text of the specified line in an Edit control.
Returns the number of lines in an Edit control.

Returns the result of raising e (which is approximately 2.71828182845905) to the Nth power.
Writes text or binary data to the end of a file (first creating the file, if necessary).
Options: Encoding = UTF-8, UTF-16, etc RAW = writes text without conversion `n = inserts CR before LF if not already provided
Copies one or more files.
Creates a shortcut (.lnk) file.

Target: The absolute path | from PATH env. (Notepad.exe). The file does not have to exist at the time the shortcut is created.
LinkFile: The absolute path | name (assumed to be in A_WorkingDir) with .lnk extension. The destination dir. must already exist!
WorkingDir: The directory that will become Target's current working directory.
Args: The parameters, separated by spaces, that will be passed to Target.
Description: Comment that describe the shortcut (used by the Windows to display a tooltip, etc.)
IconFile: The absolute path and name of the icon to be displayed for LinkFile. .ICO file | 1st icon of an EXE or DLL file.
ShortcutKey: Single letter, number, or the name of a single key from the key list without modifier symbols. All shortcut keys are created as Ctrl+Alt shortcuts: e.g. ShortcutKey = 'B' -> shortcut will be Ctrl+Alt+B.
Mouse buttons and non-standard keys not supported!
IconNumber: The number of the icon to be used in IconFile.
RunState: 1 = Normal 3 = Maximized 7 = Minimized

Target might be not the absolute path if the target file resides in one of the folders listed in the system's PATH environment variable.
The shortcut key of a newly created shortcut will have no effect unless the shortcut file resides on the desktop or somewhere in the Start Menu. If the shortcut key you choose is already in use, your new shortcut takes precedence.
Deletes one or more files.
Sets the default encoding for FileRead, Loop Read, FileAppend, and FileOpen.

Specify one of the following encodings: CP0 = The system default ANSI code page. UTF-8 = Unicode UTF-8, equivalent to CP65001. UTF-8-RAW = As above, but no byte order mark is written when a new file is created. UTF-16 = Unicode UTF-16 with little endian byte order, equivalent to CP1200. UTF-16-RAW = As above, but no byte order mark is written when a new file is created. CPnnn = A code page with numeric identifier nnn. See Code Page Identifiers ¬. nnn = A numeric code page identifier.
Returns: The previous setting (encoding).
Checks for the existence of a file or folder and returns its attributes.
Returns: R = READONLY A = ARCHIVE S = SYSTEM H = HIDDEN N = NORMAL D = DIRECTORY O = OFFLINE C = COMPRESSED T = TEMPORARY L = REPARSE_POINT (typically a symbolic link)
Reports whether a file or folder is read-only, hidden, etc.
Returns: R = READONLY A = ARCHIVE S = SYSTEM H = HIDDEN N = NORMAL D = DIRECTORY O = OFFLINE C = COMPRESSED T = TEMPORARY L = REPARSE_POINT (typically a symbolic link)
Retrieves information about a shortcut (.lnk) file, such as its target file.

&OutLinkFile: The absolute path | name (assumed to be in A_WorkingDir) with .lnk extension.
&OutDir: Working directory.
&OutArgs: The parameters, separated by spaces.
&OutDescription: Comment that describe the shortcut (used by the Windows to display a tooltip, etc.)
&OutIcon: The absolute path and name of the icon. .ICO file | 1st icon of an EXE or DLL file.
&OutIconNum: The number of the icon. This value is most often 1, which means the 1st icon.
&OutRunState: 1 = Normal 3 = Maximized 7 = Minimized
Retrieves the size of a file.
Units: B = Bytes K = Kilobytes M = Megabytes
Retrieves the datetime stamp of a file or folder.
WhichTime: M = modification time C = creation time A = last access time
Retrieves the version of a file.
Includes the specified file inside the compiled version of the script.
Moves or renames one or more files.
Opens a file to read specific content from it and/or to write new content into it.

FileOpen('*', 'r') ; for stdin FileOpen('*', 'w') ; for stdout FileOpen('**', 'w') ; for stderr Flags: r = Read w = Write a = Append h = Filename is handle Returns: File object. | Properties: |- Pos, Length, AtEOF, Encoding, Handle | Methods: |- Read, Write, ReadLine, WriteLine, ReadNum[Type], WriteNum[Type], |- RawRead, RawWrite, Seek, Close
Retrieves the contents of a file.

Filename: The absolute path to the file or name of the file to read, which is assumed to be in A_WorkingDir.
Options: Separate each option from the next with a single space or tab (e.g. '`n m5000 UTF-8'):
Encoding = encoding for non UTF-8 or UTF-16 byte order mark: CP0 = The system default ANSI code page. UTF-8 = Unicode UTF-8, equivalent to CP65001. UTF-8-RAW = As above, but no byte order mark is written when a new file is created. UTF-16 = Unicode UTF-16 with little endian byte order, equivalent to CP1200. UTF-16-RAW = As above, but no byte order mark is written when a new file is created. CPnnn = A code page with numeric identifier nnn. See Code Page Identifiers ¬. nnn = A numeric code page identifier. omitted = A_FileEncoding
m1024 = decimal/hexadecimal number of bytes to load into memory. If the file is larger than this, only its leading part is loaded. `n = Replaces any/all occurrences of carriage return & linefeed (`r`n) with linefeed (`n).
Reduces performance!

Option m1024: If this option is omitted, the entire file is loaded unless there is insufficient memory, in which case an error message is shown and the thread exits. Otherwise, replace 1024 with a decimal or hexadecimal number of bytes.
A file greater than 4 GB in size will cause a MemoryError to be thrown unless the *m option is present, in which case the leading part of the file is loaded.
A MemoryError will also be thrown if the program is unable to allocate enough memory to contain the requested amount of data.
An OSError is thrown if there was a problem opening or reading the file.

Option RAW: read the file's content as raw binary data and return a Buffer object instead of a string. This option overrides any previously specified encoding and vice versa.
The object's Size property returns the number of bytes read.
NumGet or StrGet can be used to retrieve data from the buffer.
FileRead(A_AhkPath, 'RAW') ; read unmodified contents StrGet(buf, 2, 'cp0') ; get data NumGet(buf, NumGet(buf, 0x3C, 'uint') + 4, 'ushort') ; Read machine type from COFF file header. buf.Size ; bytes count
Sends a file or directory to the recycle bin if possible, or permanently deletes it.
Empties the recycle bin.
Changes the attributes of one or more files or folders. Wildcards are supported.

Attributes: R = READONLY A = ARCHIVE S = SYSTEM H = HIDDEN N = NORMAL D = DIRECTORY O = OFFLINE C = COMPRESSED T = TEMPORARY L = REPARSE_POINT (typically a symbolic link)
Mode: D = Include directories F = Include files (default when blank) R = Recursive into subfolders
Changes the datetime stamp of one or more files or folders. Wildcards are supported.

WhichTime: M = modification time C = creation time A = last access time
Mode: D = Include directories F = Include files (default when blank) R = Recursive into subfolders
Returns the specified number rounded down to the nearest integer (without any .00 suffix).
Formats a variable number of input values according to a format string.

Any number of values can be used. The values will be organized/ordered/formatted according to the FormatStr.
FormatStr: '{[ValueIndex]:Format} ...
Any desired amount or combo of {elements} can be created, even referring to the same ValueIndex, which would be useful to display the same value formatted in a variety of different ways. If ValueIndex is blank, it refers to Value1. Text appearing outside {braces} is literal.

Format: {:10} = right aligned (default alignment), 10 characters, padding with spaces {:-10} = left aligned, 10 characters, paddnig with spaces {:010} = 10 characters, padding with zeros {:d} = signed decimal integer (ie. hex to decimal) {:u} = unsigned decimal integer {:o} = octal {:x} = lower case hex: 'abcd'' {:X} = UPPER case hex: 'ABCD' {:08X} = hex, 8 characters, padded with zeros (on left) {:.2f} = float, 2 decimal places

Flags: - = left align + = use a sign (+ or -) to prefix the output value if it is of a signed type. 0 = leading zeros are added until the minimum width is reached. # =| e, E, f, a A type:, |- forces the output value to contain a decimal point. e.g. {:#.0f} can produce 1. 0. and {:#.2f} 0.22 0.5 | o, x, X type: |- prefix any nonzero output value: 0o, 0x, 0X
Examples: {:-010} = left aligned: 1000000000 {:010} = right aligned: 0000000001 {:+d} = signed decimal: +1, -1, ... {:#x} = lower case hex. with '0x' prefix
Transforms a YYYYMMDDHH24MISS timestamp into the specified date/time format.

TimeDate = in YYYYMMDDHH24MISS format Format = formatted date or time Days = d, dd, ddd, dddd Months = M, MM, MMM, MMMM Year = y, yy, yyyy Hour = h, hh, H, HH (capital = 24 hr) Min = m, mm Sec = s, ss AM/PM = t, tt
Retrieves the name/text of a key.
Retrieves the scan code of a key.
Returns the state of the specified keyboard key, mouse or controller button.

Returns a floating-point number between 0 and 100 to indicate the stick's position as a percentage of that axis's range of motion. JoyX, JoyY, and JoyZ = The X (horizontal), Y (vertical), and Z (altitude/depth) axes of the stick. JoyU and JoyV = The 5th and 6th axes of the stick. JoyR = The rudder or 4th axis of the stick.
For example, when using Xbox Wireless/360 controllers, JoyX/JoyY is the left stick, JoyR/JoyU the right stick, JoyZ the left and right triggers, and JoyPOV the directional pad (D-pad).

Returns capabilities of the controller. JoyName = The name of the controller or its driver. JoyAxes = The number of axes. JoyButtons = The number of buttons.
JoyInfo = Provides a string with controller's capabilities: axis - Z, R, U, V; P (POV control), D (POV with limited distinct settings), C (POV control is continuous).
Example string: ZRUVPD

Returns an integer between 0 and 35900 that indicates the point-of-view (hat) control value: -1 = no angle to report 0 = forward POV 9000 (i.e. 90 degrees) = right POV 27000 (i.e. 270 degrees) = left POV 18000 (i.e. 180 degrees) = backward POV
For example, when using Xbox Wireless/360 controllers, JoyPOV is the directional pad (D-pad).
Retrieves the virtual key code of a key.
Retrieves the implementation function of a method.

Can be used to check if Name is the function: IsFunc(callback) => GetMethod(callback)
If the function/method is not found or cannot be retrieved without invoking a property getter, a MethodError is thrown.
Activates the next window in a window group that was defined with GroupAdd.
Adds a window specification to a window group, creating the group if necessary.
Closes the active window if it was just activated by GroupActivate or GroupDeactivate.
It then activates the next window in the series. It can also close all windows in a group.
Similar to GroupActivate except activates the next window not in the group.
Retrieves the GuiControl object of a GUI control associated with the specified window handle.
Retrieves the Gui object of a GUI window associated with the specified window handle.
Returns true if the specified base object is in the value's chain of base objects.
Returns true if the value has a method by this name.
Returns true if the value has a property by this name.
Specifies the criteria for subsequently created or modified hotkey variants and hotstring variants.
Specifies the criteria for subsequently created or modified hotkey variants and hotstring variants.
Specifies the criteria for subsequently created or modified hotkey variants and hotstring variants.
Specifies the criteria for subsequently created or modified hotkey variants and hotstring variants.
Specifies the criteria for subsequently created or modified hotkey variants and hotstring variants.
Creates, modifies, enables, or disables a hotkey while the script is running.

KeyName: string Action: Function Object, Hotkey (string), or On|Off|Toggle|AltTab
Options: On Off B = Enable buffering according to #MaxThreadsBuffer (B0 will disable it) Pn = Sets thread priority to n. S = Makes hotkey exempt from Suspend (S0 to disable this status) Tn = Max threads (N) to be used simultaneously (#MaxThreadsPerHotkey) In = Input level set to N
Adds an icon or picture to the specified ImageList.
Adds an icon or picture to the specified ImageList.
Creates a new ImageList that is initially empty.
Deletes the specified ImageList.
Searches a region of the screen for an image.

Options_ImageFile: A single string specifying an image file, and options (before the filename).
Options (literal asterisk (*) and name): *IconN = specified icon group N *n = number of shades of variation of color intensity *Trans[N] = N is a color, ie. 'Black', 'White', 'Red', or a hex string (0xRRGGBB or RRGGBB). Mostly used with PNG, TIFF, and GIF. Makes it easier to find a match. *wN / *hN = Width and Height to scale the spacified image, or selects icon group. If omitted usually the small icon is used (16 x 16). Use '*w0 *h0' to use the actual icon size. Use -1 to scale for h or w to scale the img and keep aspect ratio.
A Bitmap handle can be used instead of file name (HBITMAP:[Handle])
Returns: boolean, True on success
Deletes a value from a standard format .ini file.
Reads a value from a standard format .ini file.
Reads a section from a standard format .ini file.
Reads a list of section names from a standard format .ini file.
Writes a value to the .ini file.
Overwrites section in the .ini file.

Can be used to create a shortcut to a URL (instead of FileCreateShortcut): IniWrite('https://www.google.com', 'C:\My Shortcut.url', 'InternetShortcut', 'URL')
The following may be optionally added to assign an icon to the above: IniWrite(>IconFile>, 'C:\My Shortcut.url', 'InternetShortcut', 'IconFile') IniWrite(>IconIndex>, 'C:\My Shortcut.url', 'InternetShortcut', 'IconIndex') Replace >IconIndex> with the index of the icon (0 is the first icon).
Replace >IconFile> with a URL, EXE, DLL, or ICO file. Examples = 'C:\Icons.dll' 'C:\App.exe' 'https://www.somedomain.com/ShortcutIcon.ico'
Displays an input box to ask the user to enter a string.

Options: Xn Yn = X and Y coords of the InputBox gui. Wn Hn = W and H of the InputBox gui. T = Timeout in seconds. Password = Shows *** in edit box.
Returns: | obj{Value, Result} |- Value = text entered by user |- Result = OK, Cancel, Timeout
Creates an object which can be used to collect or intercept keyboard input.

Options: B = BackspaceIsUndo set to false C = CaseSensitive set to true Ix = Sets MinSendLevel to Level x (1 by default) Lx = Length of input, x chars M = Modified keystrokes such as CTRL+A thru CTRL+z are recognized and transcribed if they have corresponding ASCII characters Tx = Timeout in seconds V = Sets VisibleText and VisibleNonText to true * = Sets FindAnywhere to true E = Handle single-character end keys by character code instead of by keycode
EndKeys: Zero or more keys or {SpecialKeys} that will terminate InputHook.
MatchList: A comma-separated list of key phrases, any of which will cause the Input to be terminated.

Returns InputHookObj.
Methods: KeyOpt = Sets options for a key or list of keys. Start = Starts collecting input. Stop = Terminates the Input and sets EndReason to the word Stopped. Wait = Waits until the Input is terminated (InProgress is false).
Properties: EndKey = name of the end key which was pressed to terminate the Input. EndMods = string of the modifiers which were logically down when Input was terminated. EndReason = string indicating how Input was terminated. InProgress = 1 if the Input is in progress, otherwise 0. Input = text collected since the last time Input was started. Match = item which caused the Input to terminate.
Supports get/set: OnEnd = func. on Input is termination. OnChar = func. after a character is added to the input buffer. OnKeyDown = func. on notification-enabled key is press. OnKeyUp = func. on notification-enabled key is release.
BackspaceIsUndo = Backspace key removes the most recently pressed character from the end of the Input buffer. CaseSensitive = MatchList is case-sensitive. FindAnywhere = each match can be a substring of the input text. MinSendLevel = minimum send level of input to collect. NotifyNonText = OnKeyDown and OnKeyUp callbacks are called whenever a non-text key is pressed. Timeout = in seconds. VisibleNonText = keys which can't produce text are visible (not blocked). VisibleText = keys which produce text are visible (not blocked).
Installs or uninstalls the keyboard hook.
Installs or uninstalls the mouse hook.
Searches for a given occurrence of a string, from the left or the right.

Haystack: The string whose content is searched.
Needle: The string to search for.
CaseSense: 'On'|1 = The search is case-sensitive. The letters A-Z are considered identical to their lowercase a-z counterparts. 'Off'|0 = The search is case-insensitive.
StartingPos: 1, 2, ... = first character (from right to left). -1,-2, ... = last character (from left to right). Has no effect on the direction of the search if Occurrence is specified.
Occurrence: Number of the found Needle (by default, the 1st found Needle is considered the result). Also forces the search direction: positive = search from left to right (always). negative = search from right to left (always).
Returns: the position of an occurrence of the string Needle in the string Haystack. 1 = 1st character. 0 = not found or the absolute value of StartingPos is greater than the length of Haystack.
Error is thrown if: - Needle is '' - CaseSense is invalid - Occurrence or StartingPos is 0 or non-numeric.
Returns a non-zero number if the specified label exists in the current scope.
Returns a non-zero number if the specified value is an object.
Returns a non-zero number if the specified variable has been assigned a value.
Returns a non-zero number if the specified var ref has been assigned a value.
Displays script info and a history of the most recent keystrokes and mouse clicks.
Waits for a key or mouse/controller button to be released or pressed down.
Options: D = wait for key to be pressed L = check logical state Tx = timeout, x seconds (can be decimal)
Displays the hotkeys in use by the current script.

This function is equivalent to selecting the View->Hotkeys menu item in the main window.
If a hotkey has been disabled via the Hotkey function, it will be listed as OFF or PART ('PART' means that only some of the hotkey's variants are disabled).
If any of a hotkey's variants have a non-zero #InputLevel, the level (or minimum and maximum levels) are displayed.
If any of a hotkey's subroutines are currently running, the total number of threads is displayed for that hotkey.
Enables or disables line logging or displays the script lines most recently executed.
Displays the script's variables: their names and current contents.
Returns a list of items/rows from a ListView.

Options: Selected = returns only selected Focused = returns only focused row ColN = returns column N (row sep = `n) Count = returns number of rows Count Selected = returns total of selected rows Count Col = returns total columns
Returns the natural logarithm (base e) of the specified number.
Loads a picture from file and returns a bitmap or icon handle.

Options: Wn = Sets img hight (-1 keeps aspect ratio) Hn = Sets img width (-1 keeps aspect ratio) IconN = Indicates which icon to use (1-based) GDI+ = Use GDI+ to load the image.
&OutImageType: 0 = Bitmap (default img type if omitted) 1 = Icon 2 = Cursor
Returns: bitmap or icon handle depending on whether a picture or icon is specified and whether the &OutImageType parameter is present or not. 0 = error.

loop files, <FilePattern> [, Mode]
Modes: D = Directories F = Files R = Recursive
variables inside loop: A_LoopFileName A_LoopFileExt A_LoopFilePath A_LoopFileFullPath A_LoopFileShortPath A_LoopFileShortName A_LoopFileDir A_LoopFileTimeModified A_LoopFileTimeCreated A_LoopFileTimeAccessed A_LoopFileAttrib A_LoopFileSize A_LoopFileSizeKB A_LoopFileSizeMB
loop functions: FileGetAttrib, FileGetSize, FileGetTime, FileGetVersion, FileSetAttrib, FileSetTime

loop read, <InputFile> [, OutputFile]
variable inside loop: A_LoopReadLine
FileAppend() is used in this loop without the FileName param. This performs better than making your own loop with FileAppend(), since the file remains open after each append operation.

loop parse <String> [, DelimChars, OmitChars]
variables inside loop: A_Index A_LoopField

loop reg <KeyName> [, Mode]
Mode: K = Include keys V = include values (items) R = Recurse into subkeys
variables inside loop: A_LoopRegName A_LoopRegType A_LoopRegKey A_LoopRegTimeModified
Reg Types: REG_SZ, REG_EXPAND_SZ, REG_MULTI_SZ, REG_DWORD, REG_QWORD, REG_BINARY, REG_LINK, REG_RESOURCE_LIST, REG_FULL_RESOURCE_DESCRIPTOR, REG_RESOURCE_REQUIREMENTS_LIST, REG_DWORD_BIG_ENDIAN
Loop Functions: RegRead, RegWrite([Value]), RegDelete, RegDeleteKey, RegCreateKey
Reads a value from the registry.

KeyName: Must start with a hive, ie: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE (HKLM) HKEY_USERS (HKU) HKEY_CURRENT_USER (HKCU) HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT (HKCR) HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG (HKCC) KeyName can be omitted in a Reg Loop.
ItemName: If omitted, the key's default value will be acted on.
Default: Returns a default value you specify, instead of throwing an error when an item cannot be read.
Writes a value to the registry.

ItemValueType: REG_SZ, REG_EXPAND_SZ, REG_MULTI_SZ, REG_DWORD, or REG_BINARY KeyName: Must start with a hive, ie: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE (HKLM) HKEY_USERS (HKU) HKEY_CURRENT_USER (HKCU) HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT (HKCR) HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG (HKCC) KeyName can be omitted in a Reg Loop.
ItemName: If omitted, the key's default value will be acted on.
Returns the logarithm (base 10) of the specified number.
Trims characters from the beginning and/or end of a string.
Returns the highest number from a set of numbers.

Methods: Add, AddStandard, Check, Delete, Disable, Enable, Insert, Rename, SetColor, SetIcon Show, ToggleCheck, ToggleEnable, Uncheck
Properties: ClickCount, Default, Handle

Methods: Add, AddStandard, Check, Delete, Disable, Enable, Insert, Rename, SetColor, SetIcon Show, ToggleCheck, ToggleEnable, Uncheck
Properties: ClickCount, Default, Handle
Invokes a menu item from the menu bar of the specified window.

If WinTitle is blank, LastFoundWindow is used. Menu: name of menu, ie. 'File', 'Edit', or '&File', or position: '&0' = system menu '&1' = first menu (usually 'File') '&2' = 2nd menu SubMenus: name or position (same as Menu param)
Returns the lowest number from a set of numbers.
Modulo. Returns the remainder of a number (dividend) divided by another number (divisor).
Returns the remainder of the division operation. The sign of the result is always the same as the sign of the first parameter. If the 2nd param is zero, then a ZeroDivisionError is thrown.
Checks if the specified monitor exists and optionally retrieves its bounding coordinates.
Returns the total number of monitors.
Returns the operating system's name of the specified monitor.
Returns the number of the primary monitor.
Checks if the specified monitor exists and optionally retrieves the bounding coordinates of its working area.
Clicks or holds down a mouse button, or turns the mouse wheel. Note: The Click function is generally more flexible and easier to use.

WhichButton: Left (L), Right (R), Middle (M), X1, X2
Speed: From 0 - 100 ... 0 moves the mouse instantly.
DownOrUp: D = down (default) U = up
Relative: Specify 'R' here to make the X/Y coords relative to the current mouse position.
Clicks and holds the specified mouse button at x1 y1, drags to x2 y2, and releases button.
WhichButton: Left (L), Right (R), Middle (M), X1, X2 Speed: From 0 - 100 ... 0 moves the mouse instantly. Relative: Specify 'R' here to make the X/Y coords relative to the current mouse position.
Clicks and holds the specified mouse button at the current x y coordinate, drags to x2 y2, and releases button.
Retrieves the current position of the mouse cursor, and optionally which window and control it is hovering over.
Flags: 0 = ClassNN in &OutVarControl 1 = simpler detection method for &OutVarControl (less reliable) 2 = Stores control hwnd in &OutVarControl
Moves the mouse cursor.
Speed: From 0 - 100 ... 0 moves the mouse instantly. Relative: Specify 'R' here to make the X/Y coords relative to the current mouse position.
Displays the specified text in a small window containing one or more buttons (such as Yes and No).

Options: Owner[Handle] T[Seconds]
More Options:
Group #1 Buttons: OK: 0 OK, Cancel: 1 Abort, Retry, Ignore: 2 Yes, No, Cancel: 3 Yes, No: 4 Retry, Cancel: 5 Cancel, Try Again, Continue: 6
Returns: Yes, Cancel, No, Abort, Retry, Ignore, TryAgain, Continue, Timeout If MsgBox can't be displayed, or another weird error happens, then '' is returned.
Options group #2 Icon: Icon Hand/Stop: 16 (0x10) 'Iconx' Icon Question: 32 (0x20) 'Icon?' Icon Exclamation: 48 (0x30) 'Icon!' Icon Asterisk/Info: 64 (0x40) 'Iconi'
Options group #3 Default Button: 2nd btn: 256 (0x100) 'Default2' 3rd btn: 512 (0x200) 'Default3' 4th btn: 768 (0x300) 'Default4'
Options group #4 Modality: System Modal: 4096 (0x1000) (always on top) Task Modal: 8192 (0x2000) No Titlebar Icon: 262144 (0x40000) (... and always on top)
Options group #5 Other Options: Help Button: 16384 (0x4000) Right Justify: 524288 (0x80000) Right-to-left: 1048576 (0x100000)
Returns the binary number stored at the specified address+offset.
Types = UInt, UInt64, Int, Int64, Short, UShort, Char, UChar, Double, Float, Ptr or UPtr
Stores one or more numbers in binary format at the specified address+offset.
Types = UInt, UInt64, Int, Int64, Short, UShort, Char, UChar, Double, Float, Ptr or UPtr

ObjSetCapacity: Retrieve or set an Object's capacity to contain properties.
Returns 1 (true) if an object owns a property by the specified name.
Retrieve the number of own properties contained by an object.

Decrements an object's reference count.

Retrieve or set an Object's capacity to contain properties.
Registers a function to be called automatically whenever the clipboard's content changes.

Flag: -1 = Call before previously registered 0 = Do not call (unregister) 1 = Call after previously registered Callback: MyCallback(DataType) DataType: 0 = Clipboard is now empty. 1 = Clipboard contains text. 2 = Clipboard contains non-text
Registers a function to be called automatically whenever an unhandled error occurs.

AddRemove: 0 = Unregister callback 1 = Call after previously registered (default) -1 = Call before previously registered MyCallback(Thrown, Mode) { ... Thrown = ErrorObject Mode: 0: Allow error handling to proceed as normal. This value can also be ''. 1: Suppress the default error dialog and any remaining error callbacks -1: As above, but if Mode (the second parameter) contains the word Return, execution of the current thread is permitted to continue.
Registers a function to be called automatically whenever the script exits.

AddRemove: 0 = Unregister callback -1 = Call before previously registered 1 = Call after previously registered (default) MyCallback(ExitReason, ExitCode) { ... ExitReason = Logoff, Shutdown, Close, error, Menu, Exit, Reload, Single ExitCode = Signed 32-bit integer
Registers a function to be called automatically whenever the script receives the specified message.

MaxThreads = Max number of simultaneous threads that can use the callback at any given time. Specify zero to unregister the callback. Specify negative numbers to do the same, but call the callback before other registered callbacks. MyCallback(wParam, lParam, msg, hwnd) { ...
Returns the ordinal value (numeric character code) of the first character in the specified string.
Sends a string to the debugger (if any) for display.
Pauses the script's current thread.
1 = Pause the current thread (default if omitted) 0 = Unpauses the underlying thread -1 = Toggle the puase state on the underlying thread
Prevents the script from exiting automatically when its last thread completes.

By default, the script is not persistent. However, it becomes persistent automatically under certain conditions, specifically in common cases where the user would want it to keep running, such as when responding to hotkeys, executing timers, or displaying a GUI.
Returns: the previous setting.
Retrieves the color of the pixel at the specified X and Y coordinates.
Mode: Alt = alternate method (use if normal method doesn't work properly - 10% slower) Slow = another method to get color, may work for some full-screen apps Returns hex color string.

ColorID = hex color (0xRRGGBB) Variation = Integer (0 - 255) indicating degree of color intensity variance. Returns boolean, TRUE on success.

Forces the first11 matching process to close.
Checks if the specified process exists.
Returns the name of the specified process.
Returns the process ID (PID) of the process which created the specified process.
Returns the path of the specified process.
Changes the priority level of the first matching process.
Waits for the specified process to exist.
Waits for all matching processes to close.
Generates a pseudo-random number.
Min and Max can decimals or integers. If one param is omitted, that param is set to zero.
Creates a registry key without writing a value.
KeyName: Must start with a hive, ie: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE (HKLM) HKEY_USERS (HKU) HKEY_CURRENT_USER (HKCU) HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT (HKCR) HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG (HKCC) KeyName can be omitted in a Reg Loop.
Deletes a value from the registry.

KeyName: Must start with a hive, ie: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE (HKLM) HKEY_USERS (HKU) HKEY_CURRENT_USER (HKCU) HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT (HKCR) HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG (HKCC) KeyName can be omitted in a Reg Loop. ItemName: If omitted, the key's default value is acted on.
Deletes a subkey from the registry.
KeyName: Must start with a hive, ie: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE (HKLM) HKEY_USERS (HKU) HKEY_CURRENT_USER (HKCU) HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT (HKCR) HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG (HKCC) KeyName can be omitted in a Reg Loop.
Determines whether a string contains a pattern (regular expression).

RegExMatch() shortland: <expression> ~= NeedleRegEx
NeedleRegEx: The pattern to search for, which is a Perl-compatible regular expression (PCRE). Options (if any) must be included at the beginning of the string followed by a close-parenthesis (next page...)
&Out: Ref to the output variable in which to store a match object for each captured subpattern.
StartingPos: 1 = starts at beginning 0 = starts at end -1 = starts at last char -2 = starts second before last char
Returns: the position of the leftmost occurrence of NeedleRegEx in the Haystack. 1 = 1st character. 0 = the pattern is not found.

NeedleRegEx options: At the very beginning of a regular expression, specify zero or more of the following options followed by a close-parenthesis. Example: i) = case-insens., im) = case-insens. and multiline.
i = Case-insensitive matching m = Multiline. Specifically, it changes: 1) ^ matches immediately after all internal newlines. 2) $ matches before any newlines. E.g. m)^abc$ matches xyz`r`nabc. But without the 'm' option, it wouldn't match.
s = . matches all characters including newlines. x = Ignores whitespace characters. `n and `t are ignored. A = Matches only at the very start. D = $ matches at the very end. Ignored when the 'm' option is present. J = Duplicate named subpatterns. U = Ungreedy. ? makes an individual quantifier greedy. X = Enables PCRE features that are incompatible with Perl. S = Studies the pattern to try improve its performance. C = Enables the auto-callout mode. `a = Enables recognition of additional newline markers: `v/chr(0xB), `f/chr(0xC), NEL/chr(0x85), LS/chr(0x2028), PS/chr(0x2029) or (*BSR_UNICODE) (causes \R to match any kind of newline).
`n = Causes `n to be the only recognized newline marker. `r = Causes `r to be the only recognized newline marker.
`a `n `r affect the behavior of ^ $ .

&Out: Stores a match object for each captured subpattern in RegExMatch().
If a match is found, a match object is stored in match with the methods and properties: .Pos, .Pos[N] or .Pos(N): position of the overall match or a captured subpattern. .Len, .Len[N] or .Len(N): length of the overall match or a captured subpattern. .Name[N] or .Name(N): name of the given subpattern, if it has one. .Count: overall number of subpatterns (capturing groups). .Mark: NAME of the last encountered (*MARK:NAME), when applicable. match[], match[N]: overall match or a captured subpattern.
The object also supports enumeration (for-loop, Loop match.Count is supported).

Stores a match object for each captured subpattern in RegExMatch().
If a match is found, a match object is stored in match with the methods and properties: .Pos, .Pos[N] or .Pos(N): position of the overall match or a captured subpattern. .Len, .Len[N] or .Len(N): length of the overall match or a captured subpattern. .Name[N] or .Name(N): name of the given subpattern, if it has one. .Count: overall number of subpatterns (capturing groups). .Mark: NAME of the last encountered (*MARK:NAME), when applicable. match[], match[N]: overall match or a captured subpattern.
The object also supports enumeration (for-loop, Loop match.Count is supported).
Replaces occurrences of a pattern (regular expression) inside a string.

NeedleRegEx: The pattern to search for, which is a Perl-compatible regular expression (PCRE). Options (if any) must be included at the beginning of the string followed by a close-parenthesis (next page...)
Replacement: Must be a string, can contain $1-$9 for sub-pattern matches. For values over 9, use ${10}, ${11}, etc. Convert case with: $U{#} = uppercase $L{#} = lowercase $T{#} = title case
&OutCount: Ref to the output variable in which to store the number of replacements that occurred (0 if none)
Limit: maximum number of replacements to allow. The part of Haystack to the right of the last replacement is left unchanged.
StartingPos: 1 = starts at beginning 0 = starts at end -1 = starts at last char -2 = starts second before last char

NeedleRegEx options: At the very beginning of a regular expression, specify zero or more of the following options followed by a close-parenthesis. Example: i) = case-insens., im) = case-insens. and multiline.
i = Case-insensitive matching m = Multiline. Specifically, it changes: 1) ^ matches immediately after all internal newlines. 2) $ matches before any newlines. E.g. m)^abc$ matches xyz`r`nabc. But without the 'm' option, it wouldn't match.
s = . matches all characters including newlines. x = Ignores whitespace characters. `n and `t are ignored. A = Matches only at the very start. D = $ matches at the very end. Ignored when the 'm' option is present. J = Duplicate named subpatterns. U = Ungreedy. ? makes an individual quantifier greedy. X = Enables PCRE features that are incompatible with Perl. S = Studies the pattern to try improve its performance. C = Enables the auto-callout mode. `a = Enables recognition of additional newline markers: `v/chr(0xB), `f/chr(0xC), NEL/chr(0x85), LS/chr(0x2028), PS/chr(0x2029) or (*BSR_UNICODE) (causes \R to match any kind of newline).
`n = Causes `n to be the only recognized newline marker. `r = Causes `r to be the only recognized newline marker.
`a `n `r affect the behavior of ^ $ .
Writes a value to the registry.
Writes a value to the registry default.
Replaces the currently running instance of the script with a new one.
Returns the specified number rounded to specified decimal places.
Precision: 0 = omitted = round to nearest integer. positive = round to specified decimal places. negative = round by specified digits to the left of the decimal point.
Trims characters from the beginning and/or end of a string.
Runs an external program.

Target: The target to launch, such as a document, URL, executable file (.exe, .com, .bat, etc.), shortcut (.lnk), CLSID, or system verb. To pass parameters, add them immediately after the program or document name: Run('MyProgram.exe Param1 Param2')
WorkingDir: initial working directory to be used by the new process.
Options: Min, Max, Hide *RunAs Administrator: Run('*RunAs ' cmd)
&OutPID: Ref to the output variable in which to store the newly launched program's unique Process ID (PID).

Run itself does not interpret command-line parameters or search for the target file. Instead, it attempts to execute the target as follows:
- If no verb is specified, Target is passed as-is to the lpCommandLine parameter of CreateProcess. If Target specifies a name but not a directory, the system will search for and launch the file if it is integrated ('known'), e.g. by being contained in one of the PATH folders.
The AutoHotkey directory takes precedence over the WorkingDir. To avoid this, specify the directory; e.g. .\program.exe.
- If a verb is specified or CreateProcess fails, ShellExecuteEx is attempted. - The substring of Target ending at the first space or tab may be either a predefined verb name or an asterisk followed by a verb name. If present, the optional asterisk, verb name and a single delimiting space or tab are excluded from further consideration. Verb names containing spaces or tabs are not supported, but symbols such as hyphen are permitted.
- If a leading double-quote mark is present, the substring between that and the next double-quote mark is considered to be the target file or action. - Otherwise, the first substring which ends at a space and is either an existing file or ends in exe, bat, com, cmd or hta is considered the action. - A single delimiting space is ignored, if present, and the remainder of Target is passed as command-line parameters.
Specifies a set of user credentials to use for all subsequent Run and RunWait functions.
See Run() or RunAs() for a more reliable way to elevate scripts to Administrator.
Runs an external program and wait for it's exit.

Target: The target to launch, such as a document, URL, executable file (.exe, .com, .bat, etc.), shortcut (.lnk), CLSID, or system verb. To pass parameters, add them immediately after the program or document name: RunWait('MyProgram.exe Param1 Param2')
WorkingDir: initial working directory to be used by the new process.
Options: Min, Max, Hide *RunAs Administrator: RunWait('*RunAs ' cmd)
&OutPID: Ref to the output variable in which to store the newly launched program's unique Process ID (PID).
Returns: program's exit code (as a signed 32-bit integer)

RunWait itself does not interpret command-line parameters or search for the target file. Instead, it attempts to execute the target as follows:
- If no verb is specified, Target is passed as-is to the lpCommandLine parameter of CreateProcess. If Target specifies a name but not a directory, the system will search for and launch the file if it is integrated ('known'), e.g. by being contained in one of the PATH folders.
The AutoHotkey directory takes precedence over the WorkingDir. To avoid this, specify the directory; e.g. .\program.exe.
- If a verb is specified or CreateProcess fails, ShellExecuteEx is attempted. - The substring of Target ending at the first space or tab may be either a predefined verb name or an asterisk followed by a verb name. If present, the optional asterisk, verb name and a single delimiting space or tab are excluded from further consideration. Verb names containing spaces or tabs are not supported, but symbols such as hyphen are permitted.
- If a leading double-quote mark is present, the substring between that and the next double-quote mark is considered to be the target file or action. - Otherwise, the first substring which ends at a space and is either an existing file or ends in exe, bat, com, cmd or hta is considered the action. - A single delimiting space is ignored, if present, and the remainder of Target is passed as command-line parameters.
Sends simulated keystrokes and mouse clicks to the active window.
Sends simulated keystrokes and mouse clicks to the active window.
Sends simulated keystrokes and mouse clicks to the active window.
Controls which artificial keyboard and mouse events are ignored by hotkeys and hotstrings.

For any event generated by a script to trigger a hook hotkey or hotstring, the send level of the event (set by SendLevel()) must be higher than the input level of the hotkey or hotstring (set by #InputLevel).
The default send/input level is 0: any hotkey sent from script cannot trigger hotkey from another script, because their input/sent level are the same.
Sends a message to a window or control and waits for acknowledgement.

MsgNumber: The message number to send. To find correct message number: - visit https://www.autohotkey.com/docs/v2/misc/SendMessageList.htm - open .\AutoHotkey\MagicNumbers\Database\win32.ini To convert any magic number: - Right click -> Find magic number -> Win32
wParam, lParam: First and second integer/pointer/object with a 'Ptr' property to send with message as an additional data. Used to send refs, pointers or numbers.
ControlID top-level window/control's ClassNN, text or HWND (uniq ID, integer), or an object with a 'Hwnd' property. omitted = the message will be sent directly to the WinTitle.
Timeout: maximum number of milliseconds to wait for the target window to process the message. On timeout, a TimeoutError is thrown.
Makes Send synonymous with SendEvent or SendPlay rather than the default (SendInput).
Also makes Click and MouseMove/Click/Drag use the specified method. Mode: Event, Input (default), InputThenPlay, Play
Sends simulated keystrokes and mouse clicks to the active window.
Sends simulated keystrokes and mouse clicks to the active window.
Sets the state of CapsLock. Can also force the key to stay on or off.
Sets the delay that will occur after each control-modifying function.
DelayMs: -1 = no delay at all 0 = smallest delay possible >0 = delay in ms (default is 20)
Sets the mouse speed that will be used if unspecified in functions.

Sets the mouse speed that will be used if unspecified in Click, MouseMove, MouseClick, and MouseClickDrag.
Speed: integer between 0 (fastest) and 100. 0 = move the mouse instantly.
Sets the delay that will occur after each keystroke sent by Send or ControlSend.

DelayMs and PressDuration: Specify -1 for no delay at all, and zero for the smallest possible delay. If either of these params is omitted, the current value is not changed.
Play: If blank, applies to normal SendEvent. If "Play" is specified, applies to SendPlay.
Sets the delay that will occur after each mouse movement or click.

Delayms: Specify -1 for no delay at all, and zero for the smallest possible delay.
Play: If blank, applies to normal SendEvent. If "Play" is specified, applies to SendPlay.
Sets the state of NumLock. Can also force the key to stay on or off.
Sets the registry view used by:
RegRead, RegWrite, RegDelete, RegDeleteKey and Loop Reg. Allows them in a 32-bit script to access the 64-bit registry view and vice versa. The previous setting is returned
Sets the state of ScrollLock. Can also force the key to stay on or off.
Whether to restore the state of CapsLock after a Send.
Causes a function to be called automatically and repeatedly at a specified time interval.

Function: The function object to call.
Period: Duration/frequency of timer in milliseconds. negative = the function is called once after specified milliseconds. positive = the function is repeated. 0 = the timer is stopped. SetTimer( , 0) can be used inside a timer function to mark the timer for deletion.
Priority: Thread priority (signed 32-bit integer).
Sets the matching behavior of the WinTitle parameter in built-in functions such as WinWait.

Mode: 1 = Window's title must START with WinTitle 2 = Window's title can contain WinTitle (default) 3 = Window's title must exactly match WinTitle RegEx = WinTitle, WinText, ExcludeTitle, and ExcludeText accept RegEx Each component will be separate: "i)^untitled ahk_class i)^notepad$ ahk_pid " mypid, i)^untitled and i)^notepad$
Returns: previous match mode.

Speed: Fast = Default. Detects most windows. Slow = More thorough window searching, but slower.
Returns: previous speed.
Sets the delay that will occur after each windowing function, such as WinActivate.
DelayMs: -1 = no delay 0 = smallest delay possible >0 = delay in ms
Changes the script's current working directory.
Shuts down, restarts, or logs off the system.
Flag: (combination) 0 = Logoff 1 = Shutdown 2 = Reboot 4 = Force 8 = Power down
Returns the trigonometric sine of the specified number in radians.
Waits the specified amount of time before continuing.
Arranges a variable's contents in specified order.

Options: C1 = case sensitive C0 = case insensitive CL = caes sense based on locale CLogical = numbers in strings are considered numerical Dx = sets delimiter x (default is `n) N = numeric sort Pn = do comparison starting at char n (default = 1) R = sorts in reverse order Random = sorts in random order U = removes duplicate items Z = trailing blank items are separate items \ = sort on substring after LAST backslash (ignores N and P)

Callback: MyCallback(First, Second, Offset) { ...
First = first item to compare Second = second item to compare Offset = the offset from First to Second in the original list
Callback return value:
If First > Second, then return positive number If First = Second, then return zero If First < Second, then return negative number
Emits a tone from the PC speaker.
Frequency = Integer (37 - 32767) specifying frequency of beep (default = 523). Duration = in milliseconds (default = 150)
Retrieves a native COM interface of a sound device or component.

IID: GUID of interface {xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx}
Component: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of component, ie. 'Line In:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Device: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of device, ie. 'Speakers:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Component/Device names may be numerical, contain colons, or non-existent. If a device/component has no display name, then referring to it by index is necessary.
Retrieves a mute setting of a sound device.

Component: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of component, ie. 'Line In:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Device: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of device, ie. 'Speakers:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Component/Device names may be numerical, contain colons, or non-existent. If a device/component has no display name, then referring to it by index is necessary.
Returns: boolean, True for muted.
Retrieves the name of a sound device or component.

Component: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of component, ie. 'Line In:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Device: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of device, ie. 'Speakers:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Note: Component/Device names may be numerical, contain colons, or non-existent. If a device/component has no display name, then referring to it by index is necessary.
Return value can be ''
Retrieves a volume setting of a sound device.

Component: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of component, ie. 'Line In:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Device: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of device, ie. 'Speakers:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Component/Device names may be numerical, contain colons, or non-existent. If a device/component has no display name, then referring to it by index is necessary.
Returns: float (0.0 - 100.0)
Plays a sound, video, or other supported file type.

Filename: The name of the file to play, or a 'system sound'.
System sounds: *-1 = beep *16 = Hand (stop/error) *32 = Question *48 = Exclamation *64 = Asterisk (info) Wait: Boolean. If True, waits for the sound to play before processing next statement or line of code.
Changes a mute setting of a sound device.

NewSetting: 1 = on 0 = off -1 = toggle
Component: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of component, ie. 'Line In:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Device: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of device, ie. 'Speakers:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Component/Device names may be numerical, contain colons, or non-existent. If a device/component has no display name, then referring to it by index is necessary.
Changes a volume setting of a sound device.

NewSetting: Integer/Float (0 - 100) to set the volume. The value can have a + or - prepended which will add/subtract from current volume.
Component: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of component, ie. 'Line In:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Device: Index (1-based) or name/partial name (case sensitive) of device, ie. 'Speakers:2' If omitted or blank '', then the interface for master controls are used
Component/Device names may be numerical, contain colons, or non-existent. If a device/component has no display name, then referring to it by index is necessary.
Separates a file name or URL into its name, directory, extension, and drive.
Returns the square root of the specified number.
Retrieves the text from a standard status bar control.
Waits until a window's status bar contains the specified string.

BarText: If blank, waits for StatusBar part# to become blank. Otherwise waits for specified part# to contain specified text.
Timeout: Seconds to wait. Accepts float. If timeout is reached, then 0 is returned. PartNum: If omitted, defaults to 1. Default timeout is 2000 ms, then an OSError is thrown.
Compares two strings alphabetically.

CaseSense: True|False|Locale Logical (str or num) = numbers in strings are numerical
If String1 > String2, returns positive number If String1 = String2, returns 0 If String1 < String2, returns negative number
Copies a string from a memory address or buffer.
Optionally converts it from a given code page. Length = number of characters to read
Copies a string from a memory address or buffer.
Retrieves the count of how many characters are in a string.
Converts a string to lowercase.
Returns the current memory address of a string.
Copies a string to a memory address or buffer.

Optionally converts it to a given code page. Target = Buffer-like obj or Address
Returns: buffer size, including null terminator.
Copies a string to a memory address or buffer, optionally converting it to a given code page.
Copies a string to a memory address or buffer, optionally converting it to a given code page.
Replaces the specified substring with a new string.
Separates a string into an array of substrings using the specified delimiters.

Delimiters: specified separator, default is split every char in the string
MaxParts: -1 means all (default), otherwise the str is split the specified number of times, and the remaining substring in the last element is unsplit
Converts a string to title case.
Converts a string to uppercase.
Retrieves one or more characters from the specified position in a string.

StartingPos: 1 = starts at beginning -1 = starts at last char -2 = starts second before last char 0 = returns empty string (also when starting beyond str len)
Length: Returns specified number of chars. If negative, returns the string starting from StartPos, minus the specified number of chars from the end.
Disables or enables all or selected hotkeys and hotstrings.
1 = Suspends specified hotkeys and hotstrings (default) 0 = Re-enables specified hotkeys and hotstrings -1 = Toggles suspend state.

CaseSense: 'Locale' = rules of the current user's locale. E.g., most English and Western European locales treat not only the letters A-Z as identical to their lowercase counterparts. Locale is 1 to 8 times slower than Off depending on the nature of the strings being compared.
Each value must be an <expression> but can be a simple one such as a literal number, quoted string or variable.
Case and Default must be terminated with a colon.
Each case implicitly ends at the next Case or Default or the closing brace }
Multiple cases can be defined using comma , case Value1 [, Value2, ..., Value20]: Statements... Each case may list up to 20 values.
Retrieves dimensions of system objects, and other system properties.
Returns an array of the system's IPv4 addresses.
Returns the trigonometric tangent of the specified number.
Sets the priority or interruptibility of threads. It can also temporarily disable all timers.
Sets the priority or interruptibility of threads. It can also temporarily disable all timers.
Sets the priority or interruptibility of threads. It can also temporarily disable all timers.
Changes the script's tray icon (which is also used by GUI and dialog windows).
Shows a balloon message window or, on Windows 10 and later, a toast notification near the tray icon.
Options: Info Icon: 1 Warning Icon: 2 Error Icon: 3 Tray Icon: 4 No Sound: 16 (0x10) Largest Icon: 32 (0x20)
Trims characters from the beginning and/or end of a string.

A value can be thrown by the Throw statement or by the program when a runtime error occurs.
When a value is thrown from within a Try block or a function called by one, the following occurs:
- If there is a Catch statement which matches the class of the thrown value, execution is transferred into it. - If there is no matching Catch statement but there is a Finally statement, it is executed, but once it finishes the value is automatically thrown again. - If there is no matching Catch statement or Finally statement, the value is automatically thrown again (unless there is no Catch or Finally at all, as noted above).
If the Try statement completes without an exception being thrown (and without control being transferred elsewhere by Return, Break or similar), the Else statement is executed.
Returns the class name of a value.
Shows an always-on-top window anywhere on the screen.
WhichTooltip: number between 1 and 20. Call Tooltip() with no params to destroy the tooltip.
Enlarges a variable's holding capacity or frees its memory.
Compares two version strings.
VersionB: can begin with operator symbol: <, >, <=, >=, = Return: 0 = equal 1 = A > B -1 = A < B
Activates the specified window.
Same as WinActivate except that it activates the bottommost matching window rather than the topmost.
Checks if the specified window is active and returns its unique ID (HWND).
Closes the specified window.
Checks if the specified window exists and returns the unique ID (HWND) of the first matching window.
Retrieves the specified window's class name.
Retrieves the position and size of the specified window's client area.
Returns the control names for all controls in the specified window.
Returns the unique ID numbers for all controls in the specified window.
Returns the number of existing windows that match the specified criteria.
Returns the extended style of the specified window.
Returns the unique ID number of the specified window.
Returns the unique ID number of the last/bottommost window if there is more than one match.
Returns the unique ID numbers of all existing windows that match the specified criteria.
Returns a non-zero number if the specified window is maximized or minimized.
Returns: -1 = window is minimized 1 = window is maximized 0 = window is neither
Returns the Process ID number of the specified window.
Retrieves the position and size of the specified window.
Returns the name of the process that owns the specified window.
Returns the full path and name of the process that owns the specified window.
Returns the style of the specified window.
Retrieves the text from the specified window.
Retrieves the title of the specified window.
Returns the color that is marked transparent in the specified window.
Forces the specified window to close.
Enlarges the specified window to its maximum size.
Collapses the specified window into a button on the task bar.
Minimizes or unminimizes all windows.
Minimizes or unminimizes all windows.
Changes the position and/or size of the specified window.
Sends the specified window to the bottom of stack; that is, beneath all other windows.
Brings the specified window to the top of the stack without explicitly activating it.
Redraws the specified window.
Unminimizes or unmaximizes the specified window if it is minimized or maximized.
Makes the specified window stay on top of all other windows.
Other always-on-top windows are ignored. 1 = on 0 = off -1 = toggle
Enables or disables the specified window.
1 = on 0 = off -1 = toggle
Changes the extended style of the specified window.
Value: Add '+0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle | Value Remove '-0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle & ~Value Toggle '^0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle ^ Value
Changes the shape of the specified window to be the specified rectangle, ellipse, or polygon.
Options: Wn = width of rectangle or ellipse Hn = height of rectangle or ellipse X-Y = X/Y pairs to define a custom polygon E = makes the region an ellipse Wn and Hn must be specified
Changes the style of the specified window.
Value: Add '+0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle | Value Remove '-0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle & ~Value Toggle '^0x80' NewStyle := CurrentStyle ^ Value
Changes the title of the specified window.
Makes all pixels of the chosen color invisible inside the specified window.
Makes the specified window semi-transparent.

Integer = (0 - 255)
'Off' = '' = completely turn off transparency for a window. This is functionally identical to WinSetTransColor('Off').
Specifying 'Off' is different than specifying 255 because it may improve performance and reduce usage of system resources.
Unhides the specified window.
Waits until the specified window exists.
TimeoutSec: Integer or float. omitted = waits indefinitely.
Waits until the specified window is active or not active.
TimeoutSec: Integer or float. If omitted, waits indefinitely.
Waits until no matching windows can be found.
TimeoutSec: Integer or float. omitted = waits indefinitely.
Waits until the specified window is active or not active.
TimeoutSec: Integer or float. omitted = waits indefinitely.
Throws an error and terminates execution (if no Try statement specified).

Error Classes: MemoryError = Memory allocation failed. OSError = Win32 func call failed. TargetError = Gui target could not be found (window or control). TimeoutError = SendMessage timed out. TypeError = Unexpected type (subjective, use as desired). UnsetError = Var does not exist, or is unset / no value. - MemberError - PropertyError - MethodError - UnsetItemError ValueError = Unexpected type (subjective, use as desired). IndexError = Requested __Index[] was invalid or out of range. ZeroDivisionError
Memory allocation failed.

An internal function call to a Win32 function failed.
Message includes an error code and description generated by the operating system. OSErrors have an additional Number property which contains the error code.
Calling OSError(Code) where Code is numeric sets Number and Message based on the given OS-defined error code.
If Code is omitted, it defaults to A_LastError. E.g. OSError(5).Message returns '(5) Access is denied.'

A function failed because its target could not be found.
Message indicates what kind of target, such as a window, control, menu or status bar.
SendMessage() timed out.

An unexpected type of value was used as input for a function, property assignment, or some other operation.
Usually Message indicates the expected and actual type, and Extra contains a string representing the errant value.
An attempt was made to read/assign the value of a variable, property or item, but there was no value.
An attempt was made to read/assign the property or item, but there was no value.
An attempt was made to call the method, but there was no value.
An attempt was made to read/assign the property, but there was no value.
An attempt was made to read/assign the item, but there was no value.

An unexpected value was used as input for a function, property assignment, or some other operation.
Usually Message indicates which expectation was broken, and Extra contains a string representing the errant value.

The index parameter of an object's __Item property was invalid or out of range.
Usually Message indicates which expectation was broken, and Extra contains a string representing the errant value.
Division by zero was attempted in an expression or with the Mod function.

Changes how long the script keeps trying to access the clipboard when the first attempt fails.
Default = 1000 ms -1 = keep trying 0 = try only once

Loads a DLL or EXE file before the script starts executing. #DllLoad [*i] FileName|DirName *i = ignore failure to load (optional)
Built-in variables may be used by enclosing them in percent signs (e.g., #DllLoad '%A_ScriptDir%').
Percent signs which are not part of a valid variable reference are interpreted literally.
All built-in variables are valid, except for A_Args and built-in classes.

Sends any syntax error that prevents a script from launching to the standard error stream (stderr) rather than displaying a dialog.
#ErrorStdOut Encoding Default = CP0

Creates context-sensitive hotkeys and hotstrings. They perform a different action (or none at all) depending on any condition (an expression).
#HotIf Expression1 Enable this keys variants if Expression1 evaluates to the True. #o::... #k::... #HotIf Expression2 Enable this keys variants if Expression2 evaluates to the True. #o::... #k::... #HotIf Disable context sensitivity: all keys will work everywhere. #o::... #k::...
Sets the maximum time that may be spent evaluating a single #HotIf expression. #HotIfTimeout Timeout Default = 1000 ms

Changes hotstring options. #Hotstring NewOptions <options> Literal unquoted NewOptions, followed by string of new options.
When specifying more than one option, spaces optionally may be included between them: * = An ending character (e.g. Space or Enter) is not required to trigger the hotstring. ? = The hotstring will be triggered even when it is inside another word. C = Case-sensitive. When you type an abbreviation, it must exactly match the case defined in the script. O = Omit the ending character of auto-replace hotstrings when the replacement is produced. T = Send the replacement text using Text mode. X = Execute. Hotstring accepts a function or expression to execute. Z = Resets the hotstring recognizer after each triggering of the hotstring.
B0 = Automatic backspacing is not done to erase the abbreviation you type. KN = Sets the N delay between keystrokes produced by auto-backspacing or auto-replacement. PN = Sets the N priority of the hotstring.
Suspend: S = Specify the letter S to make the hotstring exempt from Suspend. S0 = Remove the exemption, allowing the hotstring to be suspended.
Sets the method by which auto-replace hotstrings send their keystrokes. These options are mutually exclusive: only one can be in effect at a time. SI = SendInput, which typically has superior speed and reliability than the other modes. Another benefit is that like SendPlay, SendInput postpones anything you type. SP = SendPlay, which may allow hotstrings to work in a broader variety of games. SE = SendEvent.

Changes hotstring ending characters. #Hotstring EndChars <characterss> Literal unquoted EndChars followed by a single space and then the new ending characters.
Default = -()[]{}':;"/\,.?!`n`s`t
Prevents mouse clicks from resetting the hotstring recognizer. #Hotstring NoMouse Literal unquoted NoMouse
Creates, modifies, enables, or disables a hotstring while the script is running.

Retrieves or modifies the set of ending characters. Default = -()[]{}':;"/\,.?!`n`s`t
Returns: previous chars.
Controls whether mouse clicks reset the hotstring recognizer. Returns: previous value.
Immediately resets the hotstring recognizer.
Changes hotstring options.

Changes hotstring options. When specifying more than one option, spaces optionally may be included between them: * = An ending character (e.g. Space or Enter) is not required to trigger the hotstring. ? = The hotstring will be triggered even when it is inside another word. C = Case-sensitive. When you type an abbreviation, it must exactly match the case defined in the script.
Turning on C or ? also affects which existing hotstrings will be found by any subsequent calls to the HotString().
O = Omit the ending character of auto-replace hotstrings when the replacement is produced. T = Send the replacement text using Text mode. X = Execute. Hotstring accepts a function or expression to execute. Z = Resets the hotstring recognizer after each triggering of the hotstring.
B0 = Automatic backspacing is not done to erase the abbreviation you type. KN = Sets the N delay between keystrokes produced by auto-backspacing or auto-replacement. PN = Sets the N priority of the hotstring.
Suspend: S = Specify the letter S to make the hotstring exempt from Suspend. S0 = Remove the exemption, allowing the hotstring to be suspended.
Sets the method by which auto-replace hotstrings send their keystrokes. These options are mutually exclusive: only one can be in effect at a time. SI = SendInput, which typically has superior speed and reliability than the other modes. Another benefit is that like SendPlay, SendInput postpones anything you type. SP = SendPlay, which may allow hotstrings to work in a broader variety of games. SE = SendEvent.

Copies the contents of the file to the place where directive is used. Ensures that the specified file is included only once.
#Include [*i] <Lib> *i = ignore failure to load (optional)
The library locations are searched in the order: A_ScriptDir '\Lib\' ; Local library. A_MyDocuments '\AutoHotkey\Lib\' ; User library. 'directory-of-the-currently-running-AutoHotkey.exe\Lib\' ; Standard library.

Copies the contents of the file to the place where directive is used. #Include [*i] FileName|DirName *i = ignore failure to load (optional)
Built-in variables may be used by enclosing them in percent signs (e.g., #Include '%A_ScriptDir%').
Percent signs which are not part of a valid variable reference are interpreted literally. All built-in variables containing strings or numbers are valid.

Copies the contents of the file to the place where directive is used. Allows multiple inclusions of the same file. #IncludeAgain [*i] <Lib> *i = ignore failure to load (optional)
The library locations are searched in the order: A_ScriptDir '\Lib\' ; Local library. A_MyDocuments '\AutoHotkey\Lib\' ; User library. 'directory-of-the-currently-running-AutoHotkey.exe\Lib\' ; Standard library.

Copies the contents of the file to the place where directive is used. #IncludeAgain [*i] FileName|DirName *i = ignore failure to load (optional)
Built-in variables may be used by enclosing them in percent signs (e.g., #IncludeAgain '%A_ScriptDir%').
Percent signs which are not part of a valid variable reference are interpreted literally. All built-in variables containing strings or numbers are valid.

Controls which artificial keyboard and mouse events are ignored by hotkeys and hotstrings. #InputLevel Level
For any event generated by a script to trigger a hook hotkey or hotstring, the send level of the event (set by SendLevel()) must be higher than the input level of the hotkey or hotstring (set by #InputLevel).
The default send/input level is 0: any hotkey sent from script cannot trigger hotkey from another script, because their input/sent level are the same.
Sets the maximum number of simultaneous threads. #MaxThreads Number Default = 10
Causes some or all hotkeys to buffer rather than ignore keypresses when their #MaxThreadsPerHotkey limit has been reached. #MaxThreadsBuffer Number Default = False
Sets the maximum number of simultaneous threads per hotkey or hotstring. Default = 1
Disables the showing of a tray icon. The built-in variable A_IconHidden can also be used to toggle the tray icon.

Sets the required interpreter version to run the script. #Requires AutoHotkey <version>
Literal unquoted AutoHotkey followed by one of < <= > >= = immediately followed by an optional letter 'v' and a version number: >=v2.0.19, <v1.1 followed by '32-bit' or '64-bit'.
Prepending 'v' is optional: >=2.0.19, <1.1
Examples: #Requires AutoHotkey v1.1 #Requires AutoHotkey >=v2.0.1
A_AhkVersion contains current interpreter version.

Pre-release versions are considered lower than standard releases. For example, a script that '#Requires AutoHotkey v2' will not run on 'v2.0-a112'. To permit pre-release versions, include a hyphen suffix, e.g. 'v2.0-'
If you do not wish to run scripts with v2.1-alpha by default, ensure you have installed at least v2.0.19 and set it as default in AutoHotkey Dash.

Sets a policy for multiple instances. #SingleInstance Force|Ignore|Prompt|Off
Force = Skips the dialog box and replaces the old instance automatically, which is similar in effect to the Reload() function. Ignore = Skips the dialog box and leaves the old instance running. Prompt = Displays a dialog box asking whether to keep the old instance or replace it with the new one. Off = Allows multiple instances of the script to run concurrently.
Default = Prompt

Exempts subsequent hotkeys and hotstrings from suspension. #SuspendExempt True|False
Hotkeys and hotstrings can be suspended by the Suspend function or via the tray icon or main window.
Default = True

Forces the use of the hook to implement all or some keyboard hotkeys. #UseHook True|False
Turning this directive ON is equivalent to using the $ prefix in the definition of each affected hotkey.
Default = True

Enables or disables syntax and execution warnings (strict mode). #Warn [WarningType, WarningMode]
WarnType = VarUnset, LocalSameAsGlobal, Unreachable, All WarnMode = MsgBox, StdOut, OutputDebug, Off

Skips the gentle method of activating a window and goes straight to the forceful method.
Specifying this anywhere in a script will cause built-in functions that activate a window (such as WinActivate(), WinActivateBottom() or GroupActivate()) to skip the 'gentle' method of activating a window and go straight to the more forceful methods.
Although this directive will usually not change how quickly or reliably a window is activated, it might prevent task bar buttons from flashing when different windows are activated quickly one after the other.
